rules.title: Application bundle
evidence: Application bundles should be uninstalled through an app manager or explicit user action, not bulk cleanup.
conditions: Manual uninstall only.
rules.recovery: Reinstall from the vendor or App Store if removed manually.

rules.title: User document area
evidence: User documents, media, and project files may contain unique personal work.
conditions: Manual review required.
rules.recovery: Restore from backup if removed.

rules.title: GarageBand or Logic asset
evidence: GarageBand/Logic assets can be needed for instruments, loops, or piano workflows.
conditions: Use the app's sound-library manager or manual review.
rules.recovery: Re-download from GarageBand or Logic sound library management.

rules.title: Codex credentials/config/state
evidence: Codex identity, configuration, memories, skills, goals, and state are not cleanup targets.
conditions: Never remove automatically.
rules.recovery: Restore from backup; some data may not be recoverable.

rules.title: Codex session transcript
evidence: Codex sessions can contain prompts, tool outputs, decisions, and recovery context.
conditions: Compress only after review and no open file handles.
rules.recovery: Keep compressed transcript or restore from backup.

rules.title: Codex active diagnostic log database
evidence: Codex diagnostic databases may be active and are useful for debugging app behavior.
conditions: Manual only; quit Codex and verify no open handles first.
rules.recovery: Codex may recreate logs, but historic diagnostics are lost.

rules.title: Codex rotated desktop log
evidence: Rotated Codex desktop logs are diagnostic noise compared with transcript history.
conditions: Skip current-day and open log files; keep a recent retention window.
rules.recovery: Logs are not generally recoverable after deletion.

rules.title: Codex rebuildable cache or temp data
evidence: Codex cache/temp data is rebuildable; deletion may make the next start or plugin refresh slower.
conditions: Skip files open by Codex.
rules.recovery: Codex can regenerate these caches.

rules.title: Docker or Colima runtime state
evidence: Container runtime data may include images, volumes, local databases, and VM disks.
conditions: Use Docker/Colima prune/reset commands after reviewing project dependencies.
rules.recovery: Rebuild images/volumes only if source data exists elsewhere.

rules.title: Xcode DerivedData
evidence: Xcode DerivedData contains regenerated build products and indexes.
conditions: Skip if Xcode or build processes have open handles.
rules.recovery: Xcode rebuilds DerivedData; the next build may be slower.

rules.title: Xcode module or documentation cache
evidence: Xcode module, product, and documentation caches are rebuildable developer cache data.
conditions: Quit Xcode and skip open build/index files.
rules.recovery: Xcode rebuilds these caches; the next documentation lookup or build may be slower.

rules.title: Simulator and device logs
evidence: Old simulator and device logs are diagnostic history rather than app source or project state.
conditions: Quit Simulator/Xcode and keep recent diagnostic logs if actively debugging.
rules.recovery: Logs are not generally recoverable after deletion.

rules.title: Xcode archive or device support
evidence: Archives and device support can be useful for symbolication or device debugging.
conditions: Review versions before removal.
rules.recovery: Some symbols or archives may be difficult to recreate.

rules.title: SwiftPM cache
evidence: SwiftPM dependency caches and build artifacts are usually reproducible from package manifests.
conditions: Prefer project-level clean/build commands and skip active builds.
rules.recovery: SwiftPM can fetch and rebuild dependencies when online.

rules.title: Package-manager cache
evidence: Package-manager caches are usually rebuildable, but native prune commands know their stores best.
conditions: Prefer native cleanup commands; skip active installs/builds.
rules.recovery: Packages can be re-downloaded when online.

rules.title: node_modules dependency tree
evidence: node_modules is rebuildable from lockfiles in many projects, but deleting it can interrupt local development and offline work.
conditions: Review the project and package manager before removal.
rules.recovery: Run the project package-manager install command if the lockfile and registry access are available.

rules.title: Playwright browser cache
evidence: Playwright browser binaries are large and reproducible from Playwright install commands.
conditions: Skip active test runs and expect the next Playwright install/test setup to re-download browsers.
rules.recovery: Run `npx playwright install` or the project's documented Playwright install command.

rules.title: Editor cache
evidence: Editor caches and downloaded extension packages are usually rebuildable, unlike settings, keybindings, projects, and workspaces.
conditions: Quit the editor and skip open files before removal.
rules.recovery: The editor can regenerate caches; extensions may need to be re-downloaded.

rules.title: Android or Flutter cache
evidence: Android Studio, Gradle, and Flutter package caches are often rebuildable but are best cleaned through native tooling.
conditions: Skip active builds and prefer Gradle/Flutter/Android Studio cleanup commands.
rules.recovery: Packages and build artifacts can usually be re-downloaded when online.

rules.title: Android SDK or emulator image
evidence: Android SDKs and emulator images can be large, but removing them may break installed toolchains and device targets.
conditions: Review installed SDK platforms/emulators in Android Studio or sdkmanager before removal.
rules.recovery: Reinstall SDK platforms and emulator images through Android Studio or sdkmanager.

rules.title: Browser cache
evidence: Browser cache data is usually rebuildable, unlike browser profiles, cookies, bookmarks, and history.
conditions: Quit the browser and verify no open handles.
rules.recovery: Browser cache rebuilds as sites are visited.

rules.title: Browser profile data
evidence: Browser profiles can contain bookmarks, cookies, extensions, passwords, and history.
conditions: Use browser UI or profile manager.
rules.recovery: Restore from browser sync or backup if available.

rules.title: Known stale build scratch
evidence: Known temporary build/scratch prefix under /private/tmp.
conditions: Only delete stale paths with no open handles.
rules.recovery: Temporary scratch data is expected to be recreated.

rules.title: General app log
evidence: Logs are usually diagnostic history rather than user data.
conditions: Keep recent logs and skip open files.
rules.recovery: Old logs are generally not recoverable.

rules.title: General app cache
evidence: The macOS Library Caches directory is intended for data that can be regenerated.
conditions: Skip running apps and unknown profile data.
rules.recovery: Apps recreate caches as needed.
